reasoning: This forecast is for a specific 0.05ºC bracket (1.10-1.14ºC) for the October 2025 global temperature anomaly on GISTEMP. Monthly anomalies have a standard deviation of roughly 0.08–0.10ºC around the expected trend. A 0.05ºC-wide bin covers about 0.5 standard deviations, meaning its maximum probability (if it perfectly centers on the mean) is roughly 20%, but it is generally lower if the actual mean is elsewhere. GISTEMP anomalies in late 2023 reached over 1.3ºC due to a strong El Niño and warming trend. For Oct 2025 to fall to 1.10–1.14ºC, a moderate to strong La Niña would likely need to be in place to cool global temperatures significantly compared to 2023/2024 levels. This is quite plausible and sits within the range of expected outcomes for a post-El Niño year, but it is just one of several possible 0.05ºC-wide bins (e.g., <1.10, 1.15-1.19, 1.20-1.24, etc.). Given the absence of real-time 2025 data, assigning approximately equal probability across 6–8 reasonable brackets yields a base rate of around 12% for this specific range.
